file.content:
module Api
  class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
    protect_from_forgery with: :null_session
  
    # GET /api/todoitems
    def index
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        @list_items = todo_list.todo_items
        render json: @list_items
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
      
    end

    def show
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        @list_item = todo_list.todo_items.find_by(id: params[:id])
        if @list_item
          render json: @list_item
        else
          return render json: { error: "Todo list item not found" }, status: :not_found
        end
        
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
      
    end

    def create
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        new_item = todo_list.todo_items.create(todo_item_params)
        if new_item.save
          render json: new_item
        else
          return render json: { error: new_item.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        end
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
    end

    def update
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        list_item = todo_list.todo_items.find_by(id: params[:id])
        if list_item
          if list_item.update(todo_item_params)
          render json: list_item
        else
          return render json: { error: list_item.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        end
        else
          return render json: { error: "Todo list item not found" }, status: :not_found
        end
        
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
    end

    def destroy
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        @list_item = todo_list.todo_items.find_by(id: params[:id])
        if @list_item.destroy
          render json: { success: "Todo list item deleted" }, status: :ok
        else
          return render json: { error: "Todo list item not found" }, status: :not_found
        end
        
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
      
    end

    def complete_all
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        todo_list.todo_items.each do |item|
          item.update(completed: true)
        end
        render json: todo_list.reload.todo_items
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
    end

    private

    def todo_item_params
      params.require(:todo_item).permit(:description)
    end
  end
end

content.text:
Here's a summary of all changes made:

**Routes** (`config/routes.rb`): Added `destroy` to both `todo_lists` and `todo_items` web routes.

**Controllers**:
- `TodoListsController#destroy`: Finds the list, destroys it, redirects to the index.
- `TodoItemsController#destroy`: Finds the item scoped to its list, destroys it, redirects to the list's show page.

**Views**:
- `index.html.erb`: Added a "Delete" link next to each list with the confirmation prompt _"Are you sure you want to delete this list?"_
- `show.html.erb`: Added a "Delete" link next to each item with the confirmation prompt _"Are you sure you want to delete this item?"_

Both links use `data: { turbo_method: :delete, turbo_confirm: '...' }` which is the Rails 7 / Turbo way to issue DELETE requests with a browser confirmation dialog.

class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def destroy
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    @todo_item.destroy
    redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])

class TodoListsController < ApplicationController
  # GET /todolists
  def index
    @todo_lists = TodoList.all

    respond_to :html
  end

  # GET /todolists/new
  def new
    @todo_list = TodoList.new

    respond_to :html
  end

  # POST /todolists
  def create
    @todo_list = TodoList.new(todo_list_params)

    if @todo_list.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def show
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
  end

  def destroy
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
    @todo_list.destroy
    redirect_to todo_lists_path
  end

  private

  def todo_list_params
    params.require(:todo_list).permit(:name)
  end
end

file.content:
class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id/edit
  def edit
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def update
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])

    if params[:complete]
      @todo_item.update(completed: true)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    elsif @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def destroy
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    @todo_item.destroy
    redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:description)
  end
end

toolUseResult.originalFile:
class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id/edit
  def edit
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def update
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])

    if params[:complete]
      @todo_item.update(completed: true)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    elsif @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def destroy
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    @todo_item.destroy
    redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:description)
  end
end

content.text:
**Views**:

- `_form.html.erb` partials created for both `todo_lists` and `todo_items` — they use `form_with model:` so Rails infers the correct URL and submit label ("Create Todo List" vs "Update Todo List" / "Add Item" vs "Update Todo Item") automatically.
- `new.html.erb` for both updated to render the partial.
- `edit.html.erb` created for both, also rendering the partial.
- Edit link added in `index.html.erb` (for lists) and `show.html.erb` (for items), positioned before Delete.

content.text:
Here's a summary of everything done:

**Gems**: Added `sassc-rails` and `bootstrap ~> 5.3` to the Gemfile and ran `bundle install`.

**CSS**: Renamed `application.css` → `application.scss` and replaced the sprockets manifest with `@import "bootstrap"` to pull in all Bootstrap styles.

**JS**: Pinned `popper` and `bootstrap` in `importmap.rb`, and imported `"bootstrap"` in `application.js` so Bootstrap components (dropdowns, modals, etc.) work.

**Layout**: Added a dark navbar with a "Todo Lists" brand link, and wrapped `yield` in a `container`.

**Views styled with Bootstrap**:
- `index` — `list-group` for lists, `btn-group` for actions, `btn-primary` for New List
- `show` — `list-group` for items, `text-decoration-line-through text-muted` for completed items, `btn-outline-success/secondary/danger` for Complete/Edit/Delete
- `_form` partials — `form-control` on inputs, `form-label` on labels, `btn-primary` on submit, `alert-danger` for validation errors
- All `new`/`edit` views — consistent header + back button layout

toolUseResult.originalFile:
class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id/edit
  def edit
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def update
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])

    if params[:todo_item]
      if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
        redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
      else
        render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    else
      @todo_item.update(completed: true)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def destroy
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    @todo_item.destroy
    redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:description)
  end
end

content.text:
Two changes made:

- **Controller** (`todo_items_controller.rb`): The toggle path now flips `completed` with `!@todo_item.completed` instead of always setting it to `true`.
- **View** (`show.html.erb`): The description is wrapped in a `link_to` that sends a `PATCH` request. The "Complete" button is removed. Clicking the description toggles completion — strikethrough + muted when done, normal dark text otherwise.

content.text:
Here's what changed across all views:

- **`show.html.erb`**: "Add Item" moved into the header, before "Back". The `Add Item` button at the bottom was removed.
- **`new/edit` views (lists & items)**: The submit button (`submit_tag`) is now in the header, before "Back", using the HTML `form="todo-list-form"` / `form="todo-item-form"` attribute to associate it with the form below without being inside it.
- **Form partials**: The submit button was removed from both partials since it now lives in the header of each calling view. A stable `id` was added to each form so the external submit button can target it.

Every page now has the consistent layout: `[Title]` on the left, `[Action btn] [Back]` on the right.

class TodoListsController < ApplicationController
  # GET /todolists
  def index
    @todo_lists = TodoList.all

    respond_to :html
  end

  # GET /todolists/new
  def new
    @todo_list = TodoList.new

    respond_to :html
  end

  # POST /todolists
  def create
    @todo_list = TodoList.new(todo_list_params)

    if @todo_list.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def show
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
  end

  def edit
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
  end

  def update
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])

    if @todo_list.update(todo_list_params)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def destroy
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
    @todo_list.destroy
    redirect_to todo_lists_path
  end

  private

  def todo_list_params
    params.require(:todo_list).permit(:name)
  end
end

file.content:
class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id/edit
  def edit
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def update
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])

    if params[:todo_item]
      if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
        redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
      else
        render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    else
      @todo_item.update(completed: !@todo_item.completed)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def destroy
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    @todo_item.destroy
    redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:description)
  end
end

content.text:
**Use Turbo Streams** — here's why and how they differ for your case:

---

### Turbo Frames vs Turbo Streams

**Turbo Frames** wrap a region of the page and replace it entirely on navigation/form submission. They're great for scoped in-place editing (e.g., clicking "Edit" loads the form inline). However:
- They replace the whole frame with a full server response
- Not ideal for deletion — you'd need to wrap each list item in its own frame, and the response has to render that frame's content (awkward when the record is gone)

**Turbo Streams** send one or more surgical DOM operations (`append`, `prepend`, `replace`, `remove`, etc.) targeting elements by DOM ID. For deletion, `remove` is perfect — the server just says "remove DOM element with this ID" and nothing else needs to re-render.

---

### What you need for your use case

**Single-user** (person doing the delete sees the row disappear without full reload):
Turbo Streams over HTTP — respond with a stream in the `destroy` action instead of redirecting.

**Multi-user real-time** (all connected clients see the deletion):
Turbo Streams over **ActionCable** (WebSocket broadcast) — you already have `app/channels/` scaffolded.

---

### Concrete changes needed

**1. Give each row a DOM ID** (in both views):

`index.html.erb`: `<li id="todo_list_<%= todo_list.id %>" ...>`  
`show.html.erb`: `<li id="todo_item_<%= item.id %>" ...>`

**2. In the destroy actions**, replace `redirect_to` with a stream response:

```ruby
# todo_items_controller.rb
def destroy
  @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  @todo_item.destroy
  respond_to do |format|
    format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.remove(@todo_item) }
    format.html { redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list) }
  end
end
```

`turbo_stream.remove(@todo_item)` targets `dom_id(@todo_item)` → `"todo_item_42"` — no template needed.

---

**Bottom line**: Turbo Streams with the `remove` action is the right tool. Turbo Frames would require re-rendering something into the frame slot after deletion, which doesn't make semantic sense. Want me to implement this?

message.content: how can I check or make sure that the implementation works and it is not re rendering the whole page?

content.text:
A few straightforward ways: ### 1. Browser DevTools Network tab (most reliable) Open DevTools → **Network** tab → filter by **Fetch/XHR**. When you delete a record: - **Full page reload**: you'll see a `GET` request for the full HTML page - **Turbo Stream working**: you'll see a `DELETE` request returning `text/vnd.turbo-stream.html` with a small `` payload — no follow-up GET ### 2. Check the response Content-Type In the same Network tab, click the DELETE request → **Response Headers** → look for: ``` Content-Type: text/vnd.turbo-stream.html ``` If it says `text/html` and there's a redirect, Turbo Streams isn't kicking in. ### 3. Watch the response body The response **Preview** tab should contain only a tiny fragment like: ```html ``` Not a full HTML document. ### 4. Add a visible marker to verify no re-render Temporarily add something to your layout that changes on each page load: ```erb

Page rendered at: <%= Time.now %>

``` If the timestamp doesn't update when you delete — Turbo Streams is working. If it does — it's doing a full reload. ### 5. Rails server logs In your terminal running `rails s`, a Turbo Stream delete looks like: ``` DELETE /todo_lists/1/todo_items/5 → 200 (turbo_stream) ``` vs a redirect cycle which shows: ``` DELETE /todo_lists/1/todo_items/5 → 302 GET /todo_lists/1 → 200 ``` Two requests = full re-render. One request = Turbo Stream working. --- **The Network tab + response body check is the fastest** — you can see it immediately without any code changes.

content.text:
The log tells you exactly what's happening:

- Rails **is** receiving it as `TURBO_STREAM` — good
- But the controller ignores that and does `redirect_to` anyway — that's the problem

Your `destroy` action currently has no `respond_to` block, so it always redirects regardless of format. You need to add the Turbo Stream response. Let me make the fix:

That's exactly what you want to see:

- **`Views: 0.1ms`** — essentially nothing was rendered, just the tiny `<turbo-stream action="remove">` fragment

Found it. The mismatch is:

- Your `<li>` has `id="todo_list_item_<%= item.id %>"` → e.g. `todo_list_item_5`
- `turbo_stream.remove(@todo_item)` uses Rails' `dom_id` helper → generates `todo_item_5`

These don't match, so Turbo finds nothing to remove. Fix the `<li>` id to match what `dom_id` generates:

file.content:
class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id/edit
  def edit
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def update
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])

    if params[:todo_item]
      if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
        respond_to do |format|
          format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.update(@todo_item) }
          format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path(@todo_list) }
        end
      else
        render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    else
      @todo_item.update(completed: !@todo_item.completed)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def destroy
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    @todo_item.destroy
    
    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.remove(@todo_item) }
      format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path(@todo_list) }
    end
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:description)
  end
end

content.text:
Here's what needs to happen:

**Two changes:**

**1. Extract the `<li>` into a partial** (`_todo_item.html.erb`) so the stream can re-render it. The partial is what gets sent back — Turbo replaces the old `<li>` with the freshly rendered one.

**2. Fix the `update` action** — the toggle branch (the `else` at line 39) still does a full redirect. It needs a `respond_to` block like the edit branch already has.

The `turbo_stream.replace` action needs to know what partial to render, which is why the partial is required. Without it, you'd have to write the HTML inline in the controller, which is messy.

Shall I go ahead and make both changes?

Two things done:

- **Created** `app/views/todo_items/_todo_item.html.erb` — the `<li>` row as a partial, using `dom_id(todo_item)` for the id so it matches what `turbo_stream.replace` targets
- **Updated** `show.html.erb` to use `render @todo_list.todo_items` — Rails convention that automatically uses the partial for each item

The partial uses `todo_item.todo_list` to build paths, so it doesn't depend on an `@todo_list` instance variable and can be safely rendered from a stream response.

message.content: in app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb is there anything missing to make it work?

content.text:
Two issues:

**1. The toggle branch (`else`) still redirects** — it needs a `respond_to` block, same as the edit branch already has.

**2. `turbo_stream.update` should be `turbo_stream.replace`** — `update` replaces only the *inner content* of the target element, so you'd end up with a `<li>` nested inside another `<li>` since the partial renders the full `<li>` tag. `replace` swaps out the entire element.

Both branches should end up like this:

```ruby
respond_to do |format|
  format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@todo_item) }
  format.html { redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list) }
end
```

The toggle `else` branch is the one that handles marking complete, so that's the critical fix for your current goal.

file.content:
# This file is copied to spec/ when you run 'rails generate rspec:install'
require 'spec_helper'
ENV['RAILS_ENV'] ||= 'test'
require_relative '../config/environment'
# Prevent database truncation if the environment is production
abort("The Rails environment is running in production mode!") if Rails.env.production?
require 'rspec/rails'
# Add additional requires below this line. Rails is not loaded until this point!

# Requires supporting ruby files with custom matchers and macros, etc, in
# spec/support/ and its subdirectories. Files matching `spec/**/*_spec.rb` are
# run as spec files by default. This means that files in spec/support that end
# in _spec.rb will both be required and run as specs, causing the specs to be
# run twice. It is recommended that you do not name files matching this glob to
# end with _spec.rb. You can configure this pattern with the --pattern
# option on the command line or in ~/.rspec, .rspec or `.rspec-local`.
#
# The following line is provided for convenience purposes. It has the downside
# of increasing the boot-up time by auto-requiring all files in the support
# directory. Alternatively, in the individual `*_spec.rb` files, manually
# require only the support files necessary.
#
# Dir[Rails.root.join('spec', 'support', '**', '*.rb')].sort.each { |f| require f }

# Checks for pending migrations and applies them before tests are run.
# If you are not using ActiveRecord, you can remove these lines.
begin
  ActiveRecord::Migration.maintain_test_schema!
rescue ActiveRecord::PendingMigrationError => e
  abort e.to_s.strip
end
RSpec.configure do |config|
  # Remove this line if you're not using ActiveRecord or ActiveRecord fixtures
  config.fixture_path = "#{::Rails.root}/spec/fixtures"

  # If you're not using ActiveRecord, or you'd prefer not to run each of your
  # examples within a transaction, remove the following line or assign false
  # instead of true.
  config.use_transactional_fixtures = true

  # You can uncomment this line to turn off ActiveRecord support entirely.
  # config.use_active_record = false

  # RSpec Rails can automatically mix in different behaviours to your tests
  # based on their file location, for example enabling you to call `get` and
  # `post` in specs under `spec/controllers`.
  #
  # You can disable this behaviour by removing the line below, and instead
  # explicitly tag your specs with their type, e.g.:
  #
  #     RSpec.describe UsersController, type: :controller do
  #       # ...
  #     end
  #
  # The different available types are documented in the features, such as in
  # https://relishapp.com/rspec/rspec-rails/docs
  config.infer_spec_type_from_file_location!

  # Filter lines from Rails gems in backtraces.
  config.filter_rails_from_backtrace!
  # arbitrary gems may also be filtered via:
  # config.filter_gems_from_backtrace("gem name")

  Shoulda::Matchers.configure do |config|
    config.integrate do |with|
      with.test_framework :rspec
      with.library :rails
    end
  end
end

content.text:
Two issues here:

1. `rspec-rails` is only in the `:development` group — it should also be in `:test` so it's loaded when tests run.
2. The `Shoulda::Matchers.configure` block is nested **inside** `RSpec.configure`, but it needs to be outside of it.

Fix both:

group :development do
  # Use console on exceptions pages [https://github.com/rails/web-console]
  gem "web-console"

  gem "rspec-rails"

  # Use console on exceptions pages [https://github.com/rails/web-console]
  gem "web-console"

content.text:
Both fixes resolved the error:

1. **`rspec-rails` moved to the `:test` group** — it was only in `:development`, so it wasn't loaded when running specs with `RAILS_ENV=test`.
2. **`Shoulda::Matchers.configure` moved outside `RSpec.configure`** — when nested inside, the matchers aren't integrated into example groups before examples run, so `validate_presence_of` isn't available.

The spec passes now (1 passing, 1 pending with `xit`).

content.text:
Yes, records are being cleaned — via **transactional fixtures** (`config.use_transactional_fixtures = true`, line 39).

Here's how it works: before each test, Rails wraps the entire example in a database transaction. When the test finishes, that transaction is rolled back, leaving the database in a clean state — without ever hitting `DELETE` or `TRUNCATE`.

**Caveats to be aware of:**

- **System/feature specs** (Capybara with a real browser): the browser runs in a separate thread/process that can't share the same transaction. Records written in one thread aren't visible to the other, so transactional cleanup silently breaks. If you add feature specs, you'd need `database_cleaner` with the `truncation` or `deletion` strategy for those.
- **`after(:all)` blocks**: anything persisted inside `after(:all)` or `before(:all)` runs outside the per-example transaction and won't be rolled back.

For your current setup (model/request specs without a real browser), `use_transactional_fixtures = true` is the correct and efficient approach.

file.content:
require 'rails_helper'

describe Api::TodoListsController do
  render_views

  describe 'GET index' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list, name: 'Setup RoR project') }

    context 'when format is HTML' do
      it 'raises a routing error' do
        expect {
          get :index
        }.to raise_error(ActionController::RoutingError, 'Not supported format')
      end
    end

    context 'when format is JSON' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :index, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'includes todo list records' do
        get :index, format: :json

        todo_lists = JSON.parse(response.body)

        aggregate_failures 'includes the id and name' do
          expect(todo_lists.count).to eq(1)
          expect(todo_lists[0].keys).to match_array(['id', 'name'])
          expect(todo_lists[0]['id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
          expect(todo_lists[0]['name']).to eq(todo_list.name)
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

file.content:
module Api
  class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
    protect_from_forgery with: :null_session
  
    # GET /api/todoitems
    def index
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        @list_items = todo_list.todo_items
        render json: @list_items
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
      
    end

    def show
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        @list_item = todo_list.todo_items.find_by(id: params[:id])
        if @list_item
          render json: @list_item
        else
          return render json: { error: "Todo list item not found" }, status: :not_found
        end
        
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
      
    end

    def create
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        new_item = todo_list.todo_items.create(todo_item_params)
        if new_item.save
          render json: new_item
        else
          return render json: { error: new_item.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        end
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
    end

    def update
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        list_item = todo_list.todo_items.find_by(id: params[:id])
        if list_item
          if list_item.update(todo_item_params)
          render json: list_item
        else
          return render json: { error: list_item.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
        end
        else
          return render json: { error: "Todo list item not found" }, status: :not_found
        end
        
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
    end

    def destroy
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        @list_item = todo_list.todo_items.find_by(id: params[:id])
        if @list_item.destroy
          render json: { success: "Todo list item deleted" }, status: :ok
        else
          return render json: { error: "Todo list item not found" }, status: :not_found
        end
        
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
      
    end

    def complete_all
      todo_list = TodoList.find_by(id: params[:todo_list_id])
      if todo_list
        todo_list.todo_items.each do |item|
          item.update(completed: true)
        end
        render json: todo_list.reload.todo_items
      else
        return render json: { error: "Todo list not found" }, status: :not_found
      end
    end

    private

    def todo_item_params
      params.require(:todo_item).permit(:description)
    end
  end
end

require 'rails_helper'

describe Api::TodoItemsController do
  render_views

  describe 'GET index' do
    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
      let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'returns the items belonging to the list' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        items = JSON.parse(response.body)

        aggregate_failures 'includes id and description' do
          expect(items.count).to eq(1)
          expect(items[0]['id']).to eq(todo_item.id)
          expect(items[0]['description']).to eq(todo_item.description)
          expect(items[0]['todo_list_id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end
  end
end

describe Api::TodoItemsController do

    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list, name: 'Setup RoR project') }

    context 'when format is HTML' do
      it 'raises a routing error' do
        expect {
          get :index
        }.to raise_error(ActionController::RoutingError, 'Not supported format')
      end
    end

    context 'when format is JSON' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :index, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'includes todo list records' do
        get :index, format: :json

          expect(todo_lists[0]['name']).to eq(todo_list.name)
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

require 'rails_helper'

describe Api::TodoItemsController do
  render_views

  describe 'GET index' do
    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
      let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'returns the items belonging to the list' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        items = JSON.parse(response.body)

        aggregate_failures 'includes id and description' do
          expect(items.count).to eq(1)
          expect(items[0]['id']).to eq(todo_item.id)
          expect(items[0]['description']).to eq(todo_item.description)
          expect(items[0]['todo_list_id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
          expect(items[0]['completed']).to eq(todo_item.completed)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'GET show' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'returns the item' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        item = JSON.parse(response.body)

        aggregate_failures 'includes id, description, completed' do
          expect(item['id']).to eq(todo_item.id)
          expect(item['description']).to eq(todo_item.description)
          expect(item['todo_list_id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
          expect(item['completed']).to eq(todo_item.completed)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end

    context 'when the item does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: 0 }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: 0 }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list item not found')
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'POST create' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      context 'with valid params' do
        it 'returns a success code' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }, format: :json

          expect(response.status).to eq(200)
        end

        it 'creates and returns the new item' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }, format: :json

          item = JSON.parse(response.body)

          aggregate_failures 'includes id and description' do
            expect(item['id']).to be_present
            expect(item['description']).to eq('Buy milk')
            expect(item['todo_list_id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
          end
        end
      end

      context 'without a description' do
        it 'returns a 422 status' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }, format: :json

          expect(response.status).to eq(422)
        end

        it 'returns validation errors' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }, format: :json

          body = JSON.parse(response.body)

          expect(body['error']).to include("Description can't be blank")
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        post :create, params: { todo_list_id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        post :create, params: { todo_list_id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'PUT update' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      context 'with valid params' do
        it 'returns a success code' do
          put :update, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }, format: :json

          expect(response.status).to eq(200)
        end

        it 'returns the updated item' do
          put :update, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }, format: :json

          item = JSON.parse(response.body)

          expect(item['description']).to eq('Updated')
        end
      end

      context 'with invalid params' do
        it 'returns a 422 status' do
          put :update, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }, format: :json

          expect(response.status).to eq(422)
        end

        it 'returns validation errors' do
          put :update, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }, format: :json

          body = JSON.parse(response.body)

          expect(body['error']).to include("Description can't be blank")
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        put :update, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        put :update, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end

    context 'when the item does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        put :update, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        put :update, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list item not found')
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'DELETE destroy' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        delete :destroy, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'returns a success message' do
        delete :destroy, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['success']).to eq('Todo list item deleted')
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        delete :destroy, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        delete :destroy, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end

    context 'when the item does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        delete :destroy, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: 0 }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'POST complete_all' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        post :complete_all, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'marks all items as completed' do
        post :complete_all, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        items = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(items).to all(include('completed' => true))
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        post :complete_all, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        post :complete_all, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end
  end
end

        post :complete_all, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end
  end
end

toolUseResult.originalFile:
require 'rails_helper'

describe Api::TodoItemsController do
  render_views

  describe 'GET index' do
    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
      let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'returns the items belonging to the list' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        items = JSON.parse(response.body)

        aggregate_failures 'includes id and description' do
          expect(items.count).to eq(1)
          expect(items[0]['id']).to eq(todo_item.id)
          expect(items[0]['description']).to eq(todo_item.description)
          expect(items[0]['todo_list_id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
          expect(items[0]['completed']).to eq(todo_item.completed)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end
  end
end

content.text:
Here's a summary of what was added for each action:

- **`GET show`** — happy path returns the item fields; not-found contexts for missing list and missing item.
- **`POST create`** — valid params creates and returns the item; missing description returns 422 with validation errors; missing list returns 404.
- **`PUT update`** — valid params returns updated item; blank description returns 422; not-found contexts for missing list and missing item.
- **`DELETE destroy`** — success returns 200 with message; not-found contexts for missing list and missing item.
- **`POST complete_all`** — marks all items `completed: true` and returns them; missing list returns 404.

file.content:
require 'rails_helper'

describe Api::TodoItemsController do
  render_views

  describe 'GET index' do
    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
      let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'returns the items belonging to the list' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id }, format: :json

        items = JSON.parse(response.body)

        aggregate_failures 'includes id and description' do
          expect(items.count).to eq(1)
          expect(items[0]['id']).to eq(todo_item.id)
          expect(items[0]['description']).to eq(todo_item.description)
          expect(items[0]['todo_list_id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
          expect(items[0]['completed']).to eq(todo_item.completed)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        get :index, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'GET show' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end

      it 'returns the item' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        item = JSON.parse(response.body)

        aggregate_failures 'includes id, description, completed' do
          expect(item['id']).to eq(todo_item.id)
          expect(item['description']).to eq(todo_item.description)
          expect(item['todo_list_id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
          expect(item['completed']).to eq(todo_item.completed)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end

    context 'when the item does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: 0 }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        get :show, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: 0 }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list item not found')
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'POST create' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      context 'with valid params' do
        it 'returns a success code' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }, format: :json

          expect(response.status).to eq(200)
        end

        it 'creates and returns the new item' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }, format: :json

          item = JSON.parse(response.body)

          aggregate_failures 'includes id and description' do
            expect(item['id']).to be_present
            expect(item['description']).to eq('Buy milk')
            expect(item['todo_list_id']).to eq(todo_list.id)
          end
        end
      end

      context 'without a description' do
        it 'returns a 422 status' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }, format: :json

          expect(response.status).to eq(422)
        end

        it 'returns validation errors' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }, format: :json

          body = JSON.parse(response.body)

          expect(body['error']).to include("Description can't be blank")
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        post :create, params: { todo_list_id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }, format: :json

        expect(response.status).to eq(404)
      end

      it 'returns an error message' do
        post :create, params: { todo_list_id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }, format: :json

        body = JSON.parse(response.body)

        expect(body['error']).to eq('Todo list not found')
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'PUT update' do
    let!(:todo_list) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_list) }
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      context 'with valid params' do
        it 'returns a success code' do
          put :update, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }, format: :json

          expect(response.status).to eq(200)
        end

        it 'returns the updated item' do
          put :update, params: { todo_list_id: todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }, format: :json

          item = JSON.parse(response.body)

          expect(item['description']).to eq('Updated')
        end
      end

      context 'with invalid params' do
        it 'returns a 422 status' do

class TodoListsController < ApplicationController
  # GET /todolists
  def index
    @todo_lists = TodoList.all

    respond_to :html
  end

  # GET /todolists/new
  def new
    @todo_list = TodoList.new

    respond_to :html
  end

  # POST /todolists
  def create
    @todo_list = TodoList.new(todo_list_params)

    if @todo_list.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def show
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
  end

  def edit
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
  end

  def update
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])

    if @todo_list.update(todo_list_params)
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def destroy
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
    @todo_list.destroy
    
    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.remove(@todo_list) }
      format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path }
    end
  end

  private

  def todo_list_params
    params.require(:todo_list).permit(:name)
  end
end

file.content:
class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.new(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id/edit
  def edit
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def update
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])

    if params[:todo_item]
      if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
        redirect_to todo_list_path(@todo_list)
      else
        render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    else
      @todo_item.update(completed: !@todo_item.completed)
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@todo_item) }
        format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path(@todo_list) }
      end
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/:id
  def destroy
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    @todo_item.destroy
    
    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.remove(@todo_item) }
      format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path(@todo_list) }
    end
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:description)
  end
end

Both files are empty. Now I have everything I need — writing them both.

A few things to note from the controllers:
- Both use `find` (not `find_by`), so a missing record raises `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound` → Rails returns 404.
- `TodoItemsController#update` has two paths: with `todo_item` params (edit description) and without (toggle `completed` via Turbo Stream).
- `destroy` on both controllers responds to `turbo_stream` or `html` — specs will use `format: :html`.

require 'rails_helper'

describe TodoListsController do
  before do
    @todo_list = FactoryBot.create(:todo_list)
  end

  describe 'GET index' do
    it 'returns a success code' do
      get :index

      expect(response.status).to eq(200)
    end
  end

  describe 'GET new' do
    it 'returns a success code' do
      get :new

      expect(response.status).to eq(200)
    end
  end

  describe 'POST create' do
    context 'with valid params' do
      it 'redirects to the created list' do
        post :create, params: { todo_list: { name: 'Groceries' } }

        expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_list_path(TodoList.last))
      end
    end

    context 'without a name' do
      it 'returns a 422 status' do
        post :create, params: { todo_list: { name: '' } }

        expect(response.status).to eq(422)
      end

      it 'renders the new template' do
        post :create, params: { todo_list: { name: '' } }

        expect(response).to render_template(:new)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'GET show' do
    context 'when the record exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :show, params: { id: @todo_list.id }

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end
    end

    context 'when the record does not exist' do
      it 'returns a 404 status' do
        expect {
          get :show, params: { id: 0 }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'GET edit' do
    context 'when the record exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :edit, params: { id: @todo_list.id }

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end
    end

    context 'when the record does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          get :edit, params: { id: 0 }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'PATCH update' do
    context 'when the record exists' do
      context 'with valid params' do
        it 'redirects to the updated list' do
          patch :update, params: { id: @todo_list.id, todo_list: { name: 'Updated' } }

          expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_list_path(@todo_list))
        end
      end

      context 'without a name' do
        it 'returns a 422 status' do
          patch :update, params: { id: @todo_list.id, todo_list: { name: '' } }

          expect(response.status).to eq(422)
        end

        it 'renders the edit template' do
          patch :update, params: { id: @todo_list.id, todo_list: { name: '' } }

          expect(response).to render_template(:edit)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the record does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          patch :update, params: { id: 0, todo_list: { name: 'Updated' } }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'DELETE destroy' do
    context 'when the record exists' do
      it 'redirects to the index' do
        delete :destroy, params: { id: @todo_list.id }, format: :html

        expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_lists_path)
      end
    end

    context 'when the record does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          delete :destroy, params: { id: 0 }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end
end

require 'rails_helper'

describe TodoItemsController do
  before do
    @todo_list = FactoryBot.create(:todo_list)
  end

  describe 'GET new' do
    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :new, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id }

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          get :new, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'POST create' do
    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      context 'with valid params' do
        it 'redirects to the parent list' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }

          expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_list_path(@todo_list))
        end
      end

      context 'without a description' do
        it 'returns a 422 status' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }

          expect(response.status).to eq(422)
        end

        it 'renders the new template' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }

          expect(response).to render_template(:new)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'GET edit' do
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: @todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :edit, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          get :edit, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end

    context 'when the item does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          get :edit, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: 0 }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'PATCH update' do
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: @todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      context 'with todo_item params' do
        context 'with a valid description' do
          it 'redirects to the parent list' do
            patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }

            expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_list_path(@todo_list))
          end
        end

        context 'without a description' do
          it 'returns a 422 status' do
            patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }

            expect(response.status).to eq(422)
          end

          it 'renders the edit template' do
            patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }

            expect(response).to render_template(:edit)
          end
        end
      end

      context 'without todo_item params (toggle completed)' do
        it 'redirects to the parent list' do
          patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :html

          expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_lists_path(@todo_list))
        end

        it 'toggles the completed state' do
          expect {
            patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :html
          }.to change { todo_item.reload.completed }.from(false).to(true)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do

require 'rails_helper'

describe TodoItemsController do
  before do
    @todo_list = FactoryBot.create(:todo_list)
  end

  describe 'GET new' do
    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :new, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id }

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          get :new, params: { todo_list_id: 0 }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'POST create' do
    context 'when the parent todo list exists' do
      context 'with valid params' do
        it 'redirects to the parent list' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }

          expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_list_path(@todo_list))
        end
      end

      context 'without a description' do
        it 'returns a 422 status' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }

          expect(response.status).to eq(422)
        end

        it 'renders the new template' do
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }

          expect(response).to render_template(:new)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          post :create, params: { todo_list_id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Buy milk' } }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'GET edit' do
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: @todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      it 'returns a success code' do
        get :edit, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }

        expect(response.status).to eq(200)
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          get :edit, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end

    context 'when the item does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          get :edit, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: 0 }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'PATCH update' do
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: @todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      context 'with todo_item params' do
        context 'with a valid description' do
          it 'redirects to the parent list' do
            patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }

            expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_list_path(@todo_list))
          end
        end

        context 'without a description' do
          it 'returns a 422 status' do
            patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }

            expect(response.status).to eq(422)
          end

          it 'renders the edit template' do
            patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: '' } }

            expect(response).to render_template(:edit)
          end
        end
      end

      context 'without todo_item params (toggle completed)' do
        it 'redirects to the parent list' do
          patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :html

          expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_lists_path(@todo_list))
        end

        it 'toggles the completed state' do
          expect {
            patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :html
          }.to change { todo_item.reload.completed }.from(false).to(true)
        end
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end

    context 'when the item does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          patch :update, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: 0, todo_item: { description: 'Updated' } }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end

  describe 'DELETE destroy' do
    let!(:todo_item) { FactoryBot.create(:todo_item, todo_list: @todo_list) }

    context 'when the parent todo list exists and the item exists' do
      it 'redirects to the parent list' do
        delete :destroy, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: todo_item.id }, format: :html

        expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_lists_path(@todo_list))
      end
    end

    context 'when the parent todo list does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          delete :destroy, params: { todo_list_id: 0, id: todo_item.id }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end

    context 'when the item does not exist' do
      it 'raises a record not found error' do
        expect {
          delete :destroy, params: { todo_list_id: @todo_list.id, id: 0 }
        }.to raise_error(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)
      end
    end
  end
end

content.text:
Here's a summary of what's covered in each file:

**`todo_lists_controller_spec.rb`**
- `GET index` — 200
- `GET new` — 200
- `POST create` — valid params redirects to show; blank name returns 422 and renders `new`
- `GET show` — existing record returns 200; missing raises `RecordNotFound`
- `GET edit` — existing record returns 200; missing raises `RecordNotFound`
- `PATCH update` — valid params redirects; blank name returns 422 and renders `edit`; missing raises `RecordNotFound`
- `DELETE destroy` — redirects to index; missing raises `RecordNotFound`

**`todo_items_controller_spec.rb`**
- `GET new` — 200; missing list raises `RecordNotFound`
- `POST create` — valid redirects; blank description returns 422; missing list raises `RecordNotFound`
- `GET edit` — 200; missing list or missing item raises `RecordNotFound`
- `PATCH update` — with description params (valid redirects, blank 422); without params toggles `completed` and redirects; missing list/item raises `RecordNotFound`
- `DELETE destroy` — redirects to list; missing list or item raises `RecordNotFound`

Both use a top-level `before do` block to create `@todo_list`, consistent with the API specs. `let!(:todo_item)` is kept per-describe since not every action needs an existing item.

class SyncCreateListService < ApplicationService
  def call(todo_list)
    json_list = JSON.parse(todo_list.to_json)
    json_list["source_id"] = 'dk-sys'
    json_list["items"] = todo_list.todo_items.map { |item| { source_id: 'dk-sys', description: item.description, completed: item.completed } }
    
    json_list = JSON.parse(json_list.except('id').to_json)
    CreateTodoListJob.perform_later(json_list)
  end
end

# Use JavaScript with ESM import maps [https://github.com/rails/importmap-rails]

# Hotwire's SPA-like page accelerator [https://turbo.hotwired.dev]

# Windows does not include zoneinfo files, so bundle the tzinfo-data gem

gem "bootsnap", require: false

end

  gem "rspec-rails"

  gem "selenium-webdriver"
  gem "webdrivers"

end

# This file is copied to spec/ when you run 'rails generate rspec:install'
require 'spec_helper'
ENV['RAILS_ENV'] ||= 'test'
require_relative '../config/environment'
# Prevent database truncation if the environment is production
abort("The Rails environment is running in production mode!") if Rails.env.production?
require 'rspec/rails'
# Add additional requires below this line. Rails is not loaded until this point!

# Requires supporting ruby files with custom matchers and macros, etc, in
# spec/support/ and its subdirectories. Files matching `spec/**/*_spec.rb` are
# run as spec files by default. This means that files in spec/support that end
# in _spec.rb will both be required and run as specs, causing the specs to be
# run twice. It is recommended that you do not name files matching this glob to
# end with _spec.rb. You can configure this pattern with the --pattern
# option on the command line or in ~/.rspec, .rspec or `.rspec-local`.
#
# The following line is provided for convenience purposes. It has the downside
# of increasing the boot-up time by auto-requiring all files in the support
# directory. Alternatively, in the individual `*_spec.rb` files, manually
# require only the support files necessary.
#
# Dir[Rails.root.join('spec', 'support', '**', '*.rb')].sort.each { |f| require f }

# Checks for pending migrations and applies them before tests are run.
# If you are not using ActiveRecord, you can remove these lines.
begin
  ActiveRecord::Migration.maintain_test_schema!
rescue ActiveRecord::PendingMigrationError => e
  abort e.to_s.strip
end
RSpec.configure do |config|
  # Remove this line if you're not using ActiveRecord or ActiveRecord fixtures
  config.fixture_path = "#{::Rails.root}/spec/fixtures"

  # If you're not using ActiveRecord, or you'd prefer not to run each of your
  # examples within a transaction, remove the following line or assign false
  # instead of true.
  config.use_transactional_fixtures = true

  # You can uncomment this line to turn off ActiveRecord support entirely.
  # config.use_active_record = false

  # RSpec Rails can automatically mix in different behaviours to your tests
  # based on their file location, for example enabling you to call `get` and
  # `post` in specs under `spec/controllers`.
  #
  # You can disable this behaviour by removing the line below, and instead
  # explicitly tag your specs with their type, e.g.:
  #
  #     RSpec.describe UsersController, type: :controller do
  #       # ...
  #     end
  #
  # The different available types are documented in the features, such as in
  # https://relishapp.com/rspec/rspec-rails/docs
  config.infer_spec_type_from_file_location!

  # Filter lines from Rails gems in backtraces.
  config.filter_rails_from_backtrace!
  # arbitrary gems may also be filtered via:
  # config.filter_gems_from_backtrace("gem name")
end

Shoulda::Matchers.configure do |config|
  config.integrate do |with|
    with.test_framework :rspec
    with.library :rails
  end
end

RSpec.describe CreateTodoListJob, type: :job do
  include ActiveJob::TestHelper

  let(:todo_list) { create(:todo_list) }
  let(:json_list) do
    { 'name' => todo_list.name, 'source_id' => 'dk-sys', 'items' => [] }
  end

  describe '#perform' do
    it 'is queued on the default queue' do
      expect(CreateTodoListJob.new.queue_name).to eq('default')
    end

    it 'executes without raising an error' do
      expect { CreateTodoListJob.perform_now(json_list) }.not_to raise_error
    end

    it 'can be enqueued' do
      expect { CreateTodoListJob.perform_later(json_list) }.to have_enqueued_job(CreateTodoListJob)
    end

    it 'is enqueued with the correct arguments' do
      CreateTodoListJob.perform_later(json_list)

      expect(CreateTodoListJob).to have_been_enqueued.with(json_list)
    end
  end
end

require 'rails_helper'

RSpec.describe SyncCreateListService do
  include ActiveJob::TestHelper

  let(:todo_list) { create(:todo_list) }

  describe '.call' do
    it 'enqueues a CreateTodoListJob' do
      expect { SyncCreateListService.call(todo_list) }.to have_enqueued_job(CreateTodoListJob)
    end

    it 'enqueues the job with a json payload excluding the id' do
      SyncCreateListService.call(todo_list)

      expect(CreateTodoListJob).to have_been_enqueued.with(
        hash_including('name' => todo_list.name, 'source_id' => 'dk-sys')
      )
    end

    it 'does not include the id in the enqueued payload' do
      SyncCreateListService.call(todo_list)

      expect(CreateTodoListJob).to have_been_enqueued.with(
        hash_excluding('id')
      )
    end

    it 'includes items in the enqueued payload' do
      item = create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list)

      SyncCreateListService.call(todo_list)

require 'rails_helper'

RSpec.describe SyncCreateListService do
  include ActiveJob::TestHelper

  let(:todo_list) { create(:todo_list) }

  describe '.call' do
    it 'enqueues a CreateTodoListJob' do
      expect { SyncCreateListService.call(todo_list) }.to have_enqueued_job(CreateTodoListJob)
    end

    it 'enqueues the job with a json payload excluding the id' do
      SyncCreateListService.call(todo_list)

      expect(CreateTodoListJob).to have_been_enqueued.with(
        hash_including('name' => todo_list.name, 'source_id' => 'dk-sys')
      )
    end

    it 'does not include the id in the enqueued payload' do
      SyncCreateListService.call(todo_list)

      expect(CreateTodoListJob).to have_been_enqueued.with(
        hash_excluding('id')
      )
    end

    it 'includes items in the enqueued payload' do
      item = create(:todo_item, todo_list: todo_list)

      SyncCreateListService.call(todo_list)

      expect(CreateTodoListJob).to have_been_enqueued.with(
        hash_including(
          'items' => [{ source_id: 'dk-sys', description: item.description, completed: item.completed }]
        )
      )
    end
  end
end
